app.title: Japan PR Navigator
app.tagline: Find your path to permanent residence in Japan

landing.privacy: All your data stays on this device. No account required.

trackSelect.title: How would you like to begin?
trackSelect.subtitle: Let us know whether you already know your track, or you'd like guided help finding one.
trackSelect.decidedDesc: Browse all tracks and pick yours directly — skip the questions.
trackSelect.guidanceDesc: Answer a few questions and we'll suggest the tracks that fit you best.
trackSelect.browseSubtitle: All permanent residency tracks, grouped by category. Pick the one that matches your situation.

1.title: Family of a Japanese National or PR
1.description: For spouses and children of Japanese nationals or Permanent Residents.

2.title: Long-Term Resident (定住者)
2.description: For 定住者 (Long-Term Resident) visa holders with a long, stable residence history.

3.title: Work Visa & Family Stay (10-Year Rule)
3.description: For work-visa and 家族滞在 (Family Stay) holders who meet the standard 10-year residency rule.

4.title: Highly Skilled Professional (HSP Points)
4.description: For 高度専門職 (HSP) visa holders who qualify by accumulated point score.

5.title: J-Skip (特別高度人材)
5.description: For top-tier specialists recognized under the J-Skip program — the fastest route available.

results.title: Your PR Pathways
results.subtitle: Based on your answers, here are the tracks available to you.
results.noResults: No matching tracks found. Please review your answers.
results.disclaimer: This tool provides general guidance only and does not constitute legal advice. Immigration rules change regularly. Always consult a qualified immigration lawyer (行政書士) or specialist before submitting your application.
results.lowConfidenceNote: Confidence is lower than ideal for this track. We strongly recommend consulting a qualified immigration professional to confirm your eligibility before proceeding.

vars.hsp_80_kodo_visa: Currently on 高度専門職 (80 pts)
vars.hsp_70_kodo_visa: Currently on 高度専門職 (70 pts)

hsp_1yr.title: Highly Skilled Professional – 1 Year Track
hsp_1yr.summary: For HSP visa holders with 80+ points who have lived in Japan for at least 1 year. The fastest path to permanent residence.
hsp_1yr.pro1: Fastest route — eligible after just 1 year of residence
hsp_1yr.pro2: Streamlined process for verified HSP visa holders
hsp_1yr.con1: Requires maintaining 80+ HSP points at time of application
hsp_1yr.con2: Point calculation certificate must be prepared by a registered scrivener

hsp_3yr.title: Highly Skilled Professional – 3 Year Track
hsp_3yr.summary: For HSP visa holders with 70+ points who have lived in Japan for at least 3 years.
hsp_3yr.pro1: Lower point threshold (70+) — more applicants are eligible
hsp_3yr.pro2: Well-established process with clear immigration guidelines
hsp_3yr.con1: Requires 3 continuous years on an HSP visa
hsp_3yr.con2: Point score must have been maintained throughout the residency period

long_10yr.title: Long-Term Resident Track
long_10yr.summary: For those who have legally resided in Japan for 10 or more consecutive years, demonstrating stable life in Japan.
long_10yr.pro1: Open to most legal residents regardless of visa type
long_10yr.pro2: No specialist point score calculation required
long_10yr.con1: Long wait — requires 10 consecutive years of legal residence
long_10yr.con2: Stable income and consistent tax compliance required throughout

1_1.title: Track 1-1 — Spouse of Japanese National
1_1.summary: For foreign nationals married to a Japanese citizen for 3+ years and residing in Japan for at least 1 year. One of the fastest routes if married to a Japanese national.
1_1.pro1: Relatively short residency requirement — 3-year marriage with 1 year in Japan
1_1.pro2: Spousal ties to a Japanese national are a strong eligibility factor
1_1.con1: Requires Japanese spouse's family register (戸籍謄本) and relative list
1_1.con2: Tax and social insurance proof covers the past 3 years

1_2.title: Track 1-2 — Child of Japanese National
1_2.summary: For biological or specially adopted children of Japanese nationals. The most straightforward family-track route with only 1 year of financial documentation required.
1_2.pro1: Shortest financial lookback — only 1 year of tax / pension records
1_2.pro2: No continuous residence requirement beyond being in Japan at time of application
1_2.con1: Parentage must be proved with the Japanese parent's family register
1_2.con2: Eligibility depends entirely on the parent's status as a Japanese national

1_3.title: Track 1-3 — Spouse of Permanent Resident
1_3.summary: For foreign nationals married to a Permanent Resident or Special Permanent Resident for 3+ years and residing in Japan for at least 1 year.
1_3.pro1: Follows the same streamlined document list as Track 1-1
1_3.pro2: Established process — immigration offices are familiar with this route
1_3.con1: Requires marriage certificate and a relative list (親族一覧表)
1_3.con2: Sponsor's PR status must be current and valid at time of application

1_4.title: Track 1-4 — Child of Permanent Resident
1_4.summary: For biological or specially adopted children of Permanent Residents or Special Permanent Residents. Only 1 year of financial documentation needed.
1_4.pro1: Short financial lookback — only 1 year of tax / pension records
1_4.pro2: Simple family proof: a birth certificate is typically sufficient
1_4.con1: PR parent's status must remain valid throughout the review period
1_4.con2: Children who are themselves already adults may face closer scrutiny

2_1.title: Track 2-1 — Long-Term Resident (定住者)
2_1.summary: For holders of the 定住者 (Long-Term Resident) visa who have resided in Japan under that status for 5 years or more (or 10 years total with 5 years on a qualifying visa).
2_1.pro1: Open to a broad range of nationalities eligible for 定住者 status
2_1.con1: 5-year lookback for tax, and 2-year lookback for pension/health insurance
2_1.con2: Asset documentation (bank book, real estate) must also be provided

3_1.title: Track 3-1 — Work Visa (Just Employment Route)
3_1.summary: For holders of employment-based visas (技術・人文知識・国際業務, 技能, etc.) who have lived in Japan for 10+ years, including at least 5 years on a work or residence-type visa.
3_1.pro1: No special certifications required beyond standard employment proof
3_1.pro2: Applies to a wide range of work visa categories
3_1.con1: Long requirement — 10 years of continuous legal residence in Japan
3_1.con2: 5-year tax lookback; asset and social insurance documentation required

3_2.title: Track 3-2 — Family Stay (家族滞在)
3_2.summary: For 家族滞在 (Dependent Family Stay) visa holders who have resided in Japan for 10+ years. Documentation draws on the supporting household member's income.
3_2.pro1: Viable even if the applicant has no personal income
3_2.pro2: Follows the same 10-year residency rule as Track 3-1
3_2.con1: Family relationship must be proved with an additional family status certificate
3_2.con2: 5-year tax lookback applies to the supporting household member's records

4_1.title: Track 4-1 — HSP 80 pts (ア) — Currently on 高度専門職
4_1.summary: For Highly Skilled Professionals currently residing on a 高度専門職 or 特定活動 visa with 80+ HSP points. The fastest HSP route: eligible after just 1 year.
4_1.pro1: Fastest HSP path — only 1 year of residence required
4_1.pro2: Only 1-year lookback for tax and pension documentation
4_1.con1: Must maintain 80+ points at the time of application
4_1.con2: Requires both the current and (if available) the original HSP point calculation certificates

4_2.title: Track 4-2 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Currently on Another Visa
4_2.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago but are currently residing on a visa other than 高度専門職. Requires layered documentation combining base-visa docs and HSP point sheets.
4_2.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2.con2: Base-visa documents (family, employment, etc.) must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_3.title: Track 4-3 — HSP 70 pts (ア) — Currently on 高度専門職
4_3.summary: For Highly Skilled Professionals currently on a 高度専門職 or 特定活動 visa with 70–79 HSP points. Requires 3 years of continuous residence at that point level.
4_3.pro1: Lower point threshold than Track 4-1 — 70 pts is enough
4_3.pro2: Well-established pathway with clear guidelines from the ISA
4_3.con1: Requires 3 years of continuous residence under the HSP status
4_3.con2: 3-year lookback for tax and pension documentation

4_4.title: Track 4-4 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Currently on Another Visa
4_4.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago but are currently on a non-高度専門職 visa. The most document-intensive HSP route.
4_4.pro1: Usable even if the applicant has since switched to a different visa
4_4.pro2: 70-point threshold gives access to the HSP track for a broader pool
4_4.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both now AND 3 years ago
4_4.con2: Base-visa documents stack on top of HSP materials — significant paperwork

5_1.title: Track 5-1 — J-Skip (Confirmed Recognition)
5_1.summary: For holders of the 高度専門職 visa who have received official J-Skip (特別高度人材) recognition and have resided in Japan for at least 1 year since confirmation. The fastest permanent residence route available.
5_1.pro1: Fastest route of all — only 1 year since J-Skip confirmation is required
5_1.pro2: Minimal documentation: J-Skip certificate plus 1-year financial records
5_1.con1: Requires the official 特別高度人材証明書 affixed to the passport
5_1.con2: Must continue to meet J-Skip eligibility criteria at the time of application

5_2.title: Track 5-2 — J-Skip (Unconfirmed Eligibility)
5_2.summary: For applicants who meet the J-Skip (特別高度人材) criteria now and did so 1 year ago, but have not received official J-Skip confirmation. Combines base-visa documents with a self-assessed J-Skip eligibility package.
5_2.pro1: Allows access to the J-Skip benefit period without prior formal recognition
5_2.pro2: Can be combined with most other visa types as the base
5_2.con1: No official certificate — applicant must self-document J-Skip eligibility thoroughly
5_2.con2: Higher uncertainty: ISA judgment on eligibility is discretionary without the formal stamp

4_2_1.title: Track 4-2-1 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Spouse of Japanese National
4_2_1.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a spouse-of-Japanese-national visa. Combines family relationship docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_1.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_1.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_1.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_1.con2: Family and spousal documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_2.title: Track 4-2-2 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Child of Japanese National
4_2_2.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a child-of-Japanese-national visa. Combines family docs with HSP point sheets.
4_2_2.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_2.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_2.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_2.con2: Family and spousal/child documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_3.title: Track 4-2-3 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Spouse of Permanent Resident
4_2_3.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a spouse-of-PR/SPR visa. Combines spousal family docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_3.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_3.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_3.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_3.con2: Family and spousal/child documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_4.title: Track 4-2-4 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Child of Permanent Resident
4_2_4.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a child-of-PR/SPR visa. Combines family docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_4.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_4.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_4.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_4.con2: Family and spousal/child documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_5.title: Track 4-2-5 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Long-Term Resident (定住者)
4_2_5.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a 定住者 (Long-Term Resident) visa. Combines long-term resident docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_5.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_5.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_5.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_5.con2: Long-term resident documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_6.title: Track 4-2-6 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Work Visa
4_2_6.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a work visa. Combines standard employment docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_6.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_6.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_6.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_6.con2: Employment documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_2_7.title: Track 4-2-7 — HSP 80 pts (イ) — Family Stay (家族滞在)
4_2_7.summary: For applicants who had 80+ HSP points 1 year ago and currently hold a 家族滞在 (Family Stay) visa. Combines family-stay dependent docs with HSP point evidence.
4_2_7.pro1: Still benefits from the 1-year shortened residency window
4_2_7.pro2: Accommodates applicants whose visa status changed after the HSP assessment
4_2_7.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both the current date AND 1 year ago
4_2_7.con2: Family stay supporting household documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_4_1.title: Track 4-4-1 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Spouse of Japanese National
4_4_1.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago and currently hold a spouse-of-Japanese-national visa. Combines family relationship docs with HSP point evidence.
4_4_1.pro1: Usable even if the applicant has since switched to a different visa
4_4_1.pro2: 70-point threshold gives access to the HSP track for a broader pool
4_4_1.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both now AND 3 years ago
4_4_1.con2: Family and spousal documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_4_2.title: Track 4-4-2 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Child of Japanese National

4_4_3.title: Track 4-4-3 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Spouse of Permanent Resident
4_4_3.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago and currently hold a spouse-of-PR/SPR visa. Combines spousal family docs with HSP point evidence.

4_4_4.title: Track 4-4-4 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Child of Permanent Resident
4_4_4.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago and currently hold a child-of-PR/SPR visa. Combines family docs with HSP point evidence.
4_4_4.pro1: Usable even if the applicant has since switched to a different visa

4_4_5.title: Track 4-4-5 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Long-Term Resident (定住者)
4_4_5.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago and currently hold a 定住者 (Long-Term Resident) visa. Combines long-term resident docs with HSP point evidence.

4_4_6.title: Track 4-4-6 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Work Visa
4_4_6.summary: For applicants who had 70+ HSP points 3 years ago and currently hold a work visa. Combines standard employment docs with HSP point evidence.
4_4_6.pro1: Usable even if the applicant has since switched to a different visa
4_4_6.pro2: 70-point threshold gives access to the HSP track for a broader pool
4_4_6.con1: Must submit HSP point sheets for both now AND 3 years ago
4_4_6.con2: Employment documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

4_4_7.title: Track 4-4-7 — HSP 70 pts (イ) — Family Stay (家族滞在)

4_4_7.con2: Family stay supporting household documents must also be submitted alongside HSP materials

5_2_1.title: Track 5-2-1 — J-Skip (Unconfirmed) — Spouse of Japanese National
5_2_1.summary: For J-Skip-eligible applicants without official recognition who currently hold a spouse-of-Japanese-national visa. Combines spousal family docs with self-documented J-Skip eligibility.
5_2_1.pro1: Allows access to the J-Skip benefit period without prior formal recognition
5_2_1.pro2: Can be combined with the applicant's current visa type as the base
5_2_1.con1: No official certificate — applicant must self-document J-Skip eligibility thoroughly
5_2_1.con2: Higher uncertainty: ISA judgment on eligibility is discretionary without the formal stamp

visa_type.label: What is your current visa type?

options.engineer: Engineer / Specialist in Humanities / International Services
options.spouse: Spouse of Japanese National / PR Holder

years.label: How many years have you continuously lived in Japan?

is_hsp.label: Have you ever been recognized as a Highly Skilled Professional (高度専門職)?

hsp_points.label: What is your estimated HSP point score?

employment.label: What is your current employment situation?

annual_income.label: What is your approximate annual income (JPY)?

pays_tax.label: Have you consistently paid all taxes (income tax and inhabitant tax) in Japan?

has_criminal.label: Do you have any criminal record in Japan or overseas?

hsp_point_sheet_1yr_ago.title: HSP Point Calculation Table (1 Year Ago)

hsp_point_sheet_3yr_ago.title: HSP Point Calculation Table (3 Years Ago)

residence_tax_timely_proof.title: Proof of Timely Resident Tax Payments

questmap.title: Your Progress Map
questmap.noTrack: No track selected. Please select a PR track first.

docpanel.onDevice: Stored on this device only — never sent anywhere.
docpanel.notesPlaceholder: Add notes, reminders, or reference numbers…
docpanel.dropzone: Drop a file here, or click to browse
docpanel.confirmDelete: Remove the stored file for this document?

exportImport.title: Data & Settings
exportImport.exportDesc: Download a snapshot of your progress as a JSON file. File uploads are not included.
exportImport.importDesc: Load a previously exported JSON file to restore your progress.
exportImport.importConfirm: Replace your current progress with this backup?
exportImport.importError: Could not read that file. Make sure it's a valid progress export.
exportImport.importVersionMismatch: This backup was created with a different version of the app and may not restore correctly.
exportImport.resetDesc: Permanently delete all progress, documents, and uploads from this device.
exportImport.resetConfirm: Delete all your progress and uploaded files? This cannot be undone.

j_skip_confirmed.label: Do you currently hold official J-Skip (特別高度人材) recognition issued by the Immigration Services Agency of Japan?

j_skip_1yr.label: Have you held J-Skip recognition continuously for 1 year or more, while residing in Japan on a 高度専門職 (Highly Skilled Professional) visa?

j_skip_unconfirmed_eligible.label: Although you do not hold official J-Skip confirmation, do you believe you meet the J-Skip (特別高度人材) eligibility criteria both NOW and as of 1 year ago? (You can verify the criteria on the METI website.)

hsp_points_band.label: What best describes your approximate Highly Skilled Professional (高度人材外国人) point score? You can estimate your score using the official Ministry of Justice point calculator.

opt.below_70: Below 70 points, or I have not been assessed

hsp_80_kodo_visa.label: Are you currently residing in Japan on a 高度専門職 (Highly Skilled Professional) or 特定活動 (Specified Activities designated for HSP) visa?

hsp_80_was_80_1yr.label: Was your HSP point score 80 or above as of 1 year ago?

hsp_70_kodo_visa.label: Are you currently residing in Japan on a 高度専門職 (Highly Skilled Professional) or 特定活動 (Specified Activities designated for HSP) visa?

hsp_70_was_70_3yr.label: Was your HSP point score 70 or above as of 3 years ago?

current_visa.label: What is your current visa / residence status in Japan? (Select the category that best matches your residence card.)

opt.work: Work visa — Engineer / Specialist in Humanities / International Services, Skilled Labor, Business Manager, etc.
opt.spouse_japanese: 日本人の配偶者等 — Spouse of a Japanese national
opt.child_japanese: 日本人の配偶者等 — Child (incl. specially adopted child) of a Japanese national
opt.spouse_pr: 永住者の配偶者等 — Spouse of a Permanent Resident or Special Permanent Resident
opt.child_pr: 永住者の配偶者等 — Child of a Permanent Resident or Special Permanent Resident

also_family_member.label: Are you also the spouse or child of a Japanese national, Permanent Resident, or Special Permanent Resident? (Even if your visa does not reflect this relationship.)

opt.child_japanese: Child (incl. specially adopted child) of a Japanese national
opt.spouse_pr: Spouse of a Permanent Resident or Special Permanent Resident
opt.child_pr: Child of a Permanent Resident or Special Permanent Resident
